Article Title: Supreme Court fines Raina, 4 others Rs 3 lakh each for taking court for ride
Article Text:
File photo

NEW DELHI: Samay Raina, the standup comedian who triggered national outrage and drew the Supreme Court 's ire by mocking people with disabilities, has earned the apex court's anger again: this time for not holding a show to highlight achievements of people with disabilities that he promised to conduct to make up for his original sin.The court Tuesday imposed a cost of Rs 3 lakh each on Samay Raina and four others. Senior advocate Aparajita Singh, appearing for NGO 'Cure SMA Foundation', informed a bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana that these comedians had reneged on their undertaking to the court."They have filed affidavits informing the court that they could not invite NGO members to their show as they did not have their contact details and have tried to appear magnanimous by offering donations," she said, adding Raina and others appeared to be on an "ego trip"."We do not need their donations. The NGO is a body of well-accomplished parents whose children suffer from the rare spinal muscular atrophy disease, whose treatment is very expensive. We do not want to participate in their show when they have such an arrogant attitude," Singh said on behalf of the NGO. The bench was particularly severe on Raina and accused him of lying and taking the court for a ride by brazenly violating his own undertaking to the apex court."The way he is treating persons with physical disabilities, what kind of youth icon is he?" it asked.Expressing solidarity with the NGO, solicitor general Tushar Mehta said India's youth had better icons and Raina could at best be a self-proclaimed social media influencer. "Recently, he started a show with stringed lemon and chillies and told his audience that he earlier got into trouble because he did not do this exercise as if to mock the SC," he said.The bench, which had initially slapped a fine of Rs 10 lakh on Raina, later reduced it to Rs 3 lakh and asked the other four - Vipul Goyal, Paramjeet Singh Ghai, Sonali Thakur and Nishat Jagdish Tanwar - to also pay Rs 3 lakh costs each within two weeks. The bench also warned that none of them should think that they could say and do anything sitting on foreign soil. "We had shown leniency earlier. Instead of cashing in on it, you audaciously breached the undertaking. As an artist, you are in public life and who other than you can understand what respect and dignity mean. You must respect people with disabilities and their talents," the CJI added.
Article Summary: The court Tuesday imposed a cost of Rs 3 lakh each on Samay Raina and four others. The bench accused him of lying and taking the court for a ride by brazenly violating his own undertaking to the apex court. "The way he is treating persons with physical disabilities, what kind of youth icon is he?" it asked.

Article Title: Vietnam boat survivor: Captain jumped, so did I
Article Text:
Phu Quoc chief rejects allegations of delayed rescue

‘Availability of medicines on island would’ve saved at least 6 more lives’

CHENNAI: "It all happened in the blink of an eye, and only those who were seated at the front of the speedboat and jumped into the water after the captain jumped managed to survive," Nirmal Kumar, one of the survivors of the July 11 Vietnam boat tragedy, said Tuesday as he exited the airport.Still visibly shaken, Nirmal said he had not been able to sleep since the mishap.A storm hit the boat from one side and the captain immediately jumped off. Something came over me, and I also jumped into the water. A few more followed. Only they survived. Since it was a closed boat, those seated in the rear could not get out. It was all over even as the rescue team reached the spot in 10 minutes,” Nirmal Kumar, one of the survivors of the Vietnam boat tragedy recounted.He said he lost a childhood friend, adding, “I have to go meet his family.” Nirmal’s colleagues and friends, waiting anxiously at the airport, ran forward and hugged him, with some of them breaking down.Nirmal was among a group of dealers of a mobile phone company who had gone to Vietnam on a sponsored trip. He said both Indian and Vietnam governments supported the victims, but there was a lack of immediate medical supplies at the site.Availability of medicines on the island would have saved at least six more lives, he said, adding: “We have explained this to the Vietnam government.”
Article Summary: Nirmal Kumar was among a group of dealers of a mobile phone company who had gone to Vietnam on a sponsored trip. He said both Indian and Vietnam governments supported the victims, but there was a lack of immediate medical supplies at the site. Nirmal’s colleagues and friends, waiting anxiously at the airport, ran forward and hugged him.

Article Title: EC extends deadline for voter verification exercise in Andhra Pradesh, Haryana
Article Text:
.

NEW DELHI: The Election Commission has revised the schedule for the ongoing special intensive revision (SIR) exercise in Andhra Pradesh and Haryana , allowing the electors an extra 10 days to submit their enumeration forms.As per the extended schedule, the booth level officers can continue house-to-house visits till July 24 to collect the enumeration forms. The original deadline was Tuesday.The draft roll got Andhra Pradesh and Haryana will now be published on July 31 instead of July 21. Claims and objections may be filed between July 31 and August 30 (extended from July 21 to August 20). The notice period and settlement of claims and objections will run concurrently from July 31 to September 28, instead of July 21 to September 18.The final roll for the two states will now be out on October 3 instead of September 22 as originally planned.SIR Phase 3 is currently underway in 16 states and three Union territories.
Article Summary: The Election Commission has revised the schedule for the ongoing special intensive revision (SIR) exercise in Andhra Pradesh and Haryana. As per the extended schedule, the booth level officers can continue house-to-house visits till July 24 to collect the enumeration forms. The original deadline was Tuesday.

Article Title: AR Rahman brings his magic to Washington with National Symphony Orchestra
Article Text:
AR Rahman had a electrifying performance with the National Symphony Orchestra at Wolf Trap (File photo)

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you believe that music is a universal language that transcends cultural barriers? Yes, completely No, not at all 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

AR Rahman – Headline Artist

Kevin Doucette – Management & Soloist (Piano)

Clint Valladares Global Management

Abdul Haiyum – ARR Personal Assistant

Jonathan Marius – Lighting Director

Rohith Jayaraman – Vocalist & Vocal Coordinator

Aditya Rao – Vocalist

Nivi Saishankar – Vocalist

Kiran Saishankar – Vocalist

Pragathi Guruprasad – Vocalist

Heer – Vocalist

Visveshwar Nagarajan – Bansuri / Indian Flute

Yamini Kalluri – Dancer

Harini Nilakantan – Dancer

There are moments in music when a performance becomes more than entertainment. It becomes a reminder of what humanity and genius can achieve when cultures do not compete for dominance but instead find harmony through understanding. AR Rahman’s electrifying performance with the National Symphony Orchestra at Wolf Trap arrived at precisely such a moment, on the heels of America’s 250th anniversary celebrations, offering a powerful reminder that the language of music transcends borders, politics, and geography. Rahman has long represented a rare artistic genius: an individual capable of seamlessly bringing together the complexity of Eastern musical traditions with the precision and structure of Western orchestration. Time and again, he has demonstrated that these traditions are not opposing forces but complementary expressions of human creativity. His ability to synchronize Indian classical influences, Western symphonic arrangements, contemporary sounds, and spiritual themes creates something that feels both ancient and entirely new.What makes Rahman’s brilliance distinctive is not simply his technical mastery. It is his deep understanding of what sound resonantes the soul to its core .He does not merely combine instruments or melodies; he creates conversations between cultures.. A Western orchestra does not overpower Indian rhythms. Instead, each element finds its place in a larger musical story. In this performance the vocals of the brilliant singers merged into the symphony as easily as the Bansuri flute.To appreciate AR Rahman’s genius is to understand that he is not simply composing music. He is orchestrating civilizations. Few musicians possess the depth of musical knowledge required to weave the grandeur of a Western symphony together with the intricate jathis of Bharatanatyam and Kuchipudi , the mathematical precision of Indian rhythm, the expressive beauty of swaras, and the profound spirituality that lies at the heart of Indian classical tradition. I have seen the Kuchipudi tarangam(brass plate) performed by the greatest of dancers but never with the National Symphony.Each of these disciplines demands a lifetime of mastery on its own. Rahman moves effortlessly among them, allowing orchestral strings, Indian percussion, classical dance, devotional expression, and contemporary sound to coexist as though they were always meant to belong together. He crosses languages, musical systems, rhythms, and artistic forms without ever making the transitions feel deliberate. The audience does not experience a fusion of East and West but a single, richly woven tapestry where every note, every movement, and every silence serves a larger artistic vision. That ability to unite complexity with simplicity, tradition with innovation, and spirituality with universal human emotion is what separates a gifted composer from a musical visionary.The Wolf Trap performance was a testament to this philosophy. Rahman did not make the evening about himself alone. One of the most remarkable aspects of his leadership was the way he created opportunities for the exceptional performers , all from the US (but one)around him to shine. He introduced musicians and vocalists with extraordinary talent and allowed them moments of individual expression. Great artists often reveal themselves not by standing alone in the spotlight, but by knowing when to share it.That spirit reflected the larger message of the evening. Diplomacy is often discussed through the lens of governments, treaties, and negotiations. But the most powerful diplomacy frequently occurs without a single word being spoken. Music reaches places that political language cannot. It creates emotional connections before intellectual agreements are ever reached. In an increasingly divided world, Rahman demonstrated that harmony is not merely a musical concept; it is a human aspiration.The performance also showcased Rahman’s ability to incorporate the elegance of classical dance movement into a symphonic setting. The choreography did not feel like an addition layered onto the music. Instead, movement became another instrument, another voice within the composition. The grace and simplicity of the dancers reflected the emotional architecture of the music itself, allowing the audience to experience rhythm not only through sound but through motion.One of the most profound moments of the evening was the excerpt from the Ramayan project with Hans Zimmer. The brilliance of that composition was its restraint. It captured the essence, spirituality, and emotional depth of the Ramayan without unnecessary ornamentation or theatrical excess. The power came from its simplicity. The music allowed the story to breathe, demonstrating that great art does not need to overwhelm the senses; it needs to reveal something timeless.The Ramayan has endured for thousands of years because it speaks to universal themes: duty, sacrifice, courage, compassion, and the struggle between good and evil. Rahman’s interpretation recognized that universality. Rather than attempting to recreate every detail, he distilled the emotional core of the epic and translated it into a language that audiences across cultures could understand. The dancers condensed this epic into a few succinct adavus( postures) and mudras (hand gestures) with simplicity and precision.At a time when the world often emphasizes differences, Rahman’s performance offered a different vision. It showed that identity does not have to be preserved through separation. Instead, cultures become richer when they engage, exchange, and inspire one another.America’s story itself has always been shaped by this idea of convergence: different languages, traditions, and perspectives coming together to create something new. Rahman’s performance at Wolf Trap reflected that same spirit. It was not simply an Indian artist performing for an American audience. It was a global artist demonstrating that creativity belongs to everyone.The loudest form of diplomacy is not always spoken from podiums. Sometimes it comes from a stage, through a melody, carried by instruments and voices that remind us of our shared humanity.On this evening at Wolf Trap, AR Rahman did exactly that. He did not just conduct music. He conducted connection.The performers along with the National Symphony(By arrangement with American Bazaar
Article Summary: Rahman has long represented a rare artistic genius. Rahman seamlessly brings together the complexity of Eastern musical traditions with the precision and structure of Western orchestration. Rahman moves effortlessly among orchestral strings, Indian percussion, classical dance, devotional expression, and contemporary sound. Rahman did not make the evening about himself alone.

Article Title: Kargil War: How July 14 turned the tide and put Pakistan on back foot during Operation Vijay
Article Text:
Picture from Fire Fury Corps X handle

NEW DELHI: The Army on Tuesday marked a key moment in India's military history, recalling July 14, 1999, as the decisive turning point of the Kargil War It was the day India's military campaign gained a decisive edge, forcing Pakistan to announce the withdrawal of its forces after suffering major setbacks.In a post on X, the Fire and Fury Corps of the Indian Army said, "Operation VIJAY: India Declares Victory, Pakistan Announces Withdrawal. By 14 Jul 1999, the Indian Army had regained control of most of the strategic heights in the Dras, Kargil and Batalik sectors through exceptional courage, relentless determination and meticulously coordinated operations."It added that the enemy's plans had collapsed under the sustained offensive of the Indian Armed Forces, backed by "devastating artillery fire and determined infantry assaults."Recalling the events of July 14, 1999, the Army said, "On 14 Jul 1999, the then Prime Minister Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee addressed the Nation and declared Operation Vijay a success, paying tribute to the valour, supreme sacrifice and unwavering resolve of the Armed Forces."The Army further mentioned that on the same day, "under mounting military setbacks and increasing international diplomatic pressure, Pakistan announced the withdrawal of its forces."It added that although operations continued to remove the remaining intruders, "14 Jul 1999 marked the decisive turning point that affirmed India's victory in the Kargil War. India remembers with pride and gratitude."The Army also commemorated the day by honouring "the gunners who turned the tide."As part of the 27th anniversary of Operation Vijay, the Army also organised an expedition to Gun Hill (Point 5140), a strategically important peak overlooking Dras. The event was held to honour the courage, sacrifice and professionalism of the soldiers who fought in the Kargil War.Point 5140 was one of the most important objectives during Operation Vijay because it was a major enemy position and observation post. Capturing it was crucial for regaining control of the Dras sector and stopping the enemy from dominating the surrounding heights.The Battle of Point 5140 is considered one of the most important battles of the Kargil War. Before the main assault, Indian Army troops captured nearby positions, including 'Hump', which was secured by 18 Grenadiers during the night of June 13-14, 1999. Another key feature, 'Rocky Knob', was captured by 13 Jammu and Kashmir Rifles with support from direct Bofors gun fire, which destroyed enemy bunkers and helped clear the way for the final assault.Every year on July 26, India observes Kargil Vijay Diwas to mark the successful completion of Operation Vijay, through which the Indian Armed Forces recaptured strategic heights from Pakistani intruders and protected the country's territorial integrity.
Article Summary: July 14, 1999, was the decisive turning point of the Kargil War. It was the day India's military campaign gained a decisive edge. Pakistan announced the withdrawal of its forces after suffering major setbacks. The Indian Army commemorated the day by honouring "the gunners who turned the tide"

Bronny James, 21, remains under contract with the Los Angeles Lakers on a guaranteed $2.3 million salary for 2026-27, even after father LeBron James informed the team he won't return following eight seasons. The Athletic's Dan Woike reports the Lakers view Bronny's roster spot as independent of LeBron's decision. Bronny attended the Lakers Summer League and is part of the team's veteran minicamp, which began this week.LeBron James spent eight seasons wearing purple and gold, but Bronny James might outlast him there. The younger James remains under contract with the Los Angeles Lakers even after his father informed the franchise he plans to sign elsewhere for the 2026-27 season.It has scrambled the assumption that Bronny would simply follow LeBron out the door. Multiple reports now suggest the Lakers see Bronny's future as separate from his father's decision entirely, and the front office's actions back that up.No trade has materialized, and the Lakers already made a defining move: they declined to waive Bronny on June 29, locking in his salary guarantee one day before LeBron's departure became official.Front Office Sports reported that rival executives have discussed acquiring Bronny as a potential sweetener to help lure LeBron, but that interest reflects other franchises' internal conversations, not the Lakers' intentions.The Athletic's Dan Woike has been the most direct voice cutting against the trade chatter. He described the assumption that father and son will stay linked going forward as false, based on league sources briefed on Lakers front-office strategy.Woike also noted that Bronny might be too advanced for the G League as he enters his third professional season. One Western Conference executive told Front Office Sports, "We wouldn't be doing our jobs if we didn't discuss it," when asked about trade interest in Bronny.Bronny's inclusion in the Lakers' veteran minicamp, rather than being parked exclusively with the Summer League roster, is the clearest signal of intent. Teams don't typically fold a player they plan to waive or flip into their development program for the coming season.Bronny also improved defensively and expanded his shooting sample size across his first two years, giving the coaching staff reason to keep charting his progress rather than moving him for a marginal asset. With a crowded backcourt now including Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves, Quentin Grimes, Collin Sexton and rookie Cameron Carr, the Lakers still view him as a low-cost depth piece worth developing rather than discarding.Bronny's rookie-scale deal makes him one of the cheapest roster spots the Lakers control, which matters for a front office still managing its books after guaranteeing Austin Reaves a four-year, $180 million extension this offseason. A cost-controlled guard with team-option flexibility through 2028 is easy to hold onto even without a father-son storyline attached.LeBron has not confirmed his next destination, though Cleveland has emerged as the reported frontrunner for a third stint with the franchise. Until he signs, the Lakers have no obligation to make a final call on Bronny, and nothing reported so far suggests they're in a hurry to.
Article Summary: Bronny James, 21, remains under contract with the Los Angeles Lakers on a guaranteed $2.3 million salary. LeBron James informed the team he won't return following eight seasons. The Athletic's Dan Woike reports the Lakers view Bronny's roster spot as independent of LeBron's decision.

Article Title: “Three boyfriends in the last eight years": Jude Bellingham's girlfriend Ashlyn Castro's reported Michael B. Jordan romance resurfaces
Article Text:
Jude Bellingham's girlfriend, Ashlyn Castro, has gained attention during the 2026 World Cup, but she was already familiar to celebrity watchers through her reported romance with Michael B. Jordan in 2017. The American model and influencer was later linked to Bellingham in 2025. She has also denied dating rumors surrounding her personal life, insisting she has had only "three boyfriends over the last eight years" despite ongoing online speculation.

Ashlyn Castro's reported Michael B. Jordan romance put her in the spotlight

Ashlyn Castro and Jude Bellingham continue to attract attention during the World Cup

Jude Bellingham’s World Cup performances have kept football fans talking but the England star is not the only one drawing attention. His girlfriend, American model Ashlyn Castro, has been a familiar face in the crowd, cheering him on during the tournament. Her romance with Bellingham continues to make headlines, but Castro was already a celebrity in celebrity circles years before meeting him, following a reported romance with Hollywood star Michael B. Jordan Before her association with Jude Bellingham, Ashlyn Castro built a career as a model, influencer and content creator. She was born in California on December 17, 1997 and is 28 years old and has worked with big fashion brands like Pretty Little Thing and appeared in DJ Khaled’s 2021 music video for Popstar. Her social media presence has also grown, amassing more than 650,000 followers on Instagram.But one of the first moments that catapulted Castro into the celebrity spotlight happened at the end of 2017 when she was linked to Oscar-winning actor Michael B. Jordan.The pair were photographed together at a private event in Los Angeles, with Castro sitting on the actor's lap. Reports later claimed they also spent New Year's Eve together before eventually going their separate ways in late 2018.Neither publicly discussed the relationship in detail, but the speculation introduced Castro to entertainment headlines well before football fans knew her name.Since then, she has largely focused on her career while maintaining a relatively private personal life despite growing public interest.Castro and Bellingham were first linked in early 2025 after she was spotted watching a Real Madrid match alongside the footballer's parents. Although reports suggested the pair met through the celebrity dating app Raya, neither has confirmed how their relationship began.A source previously told The Mirror that Castro was well prepared for the attention that comes with dating one of football's biggest stars because of her previous experience around celebrities. According to the source, "With several years of experience in the world of A-listers, she understands how to maintain intrigue while keeping her private life private."Her relationship has not escaped online rumors. Earlier this year, social media users speculated that Castro had been unfaithful to Bellingham after she was linked to Atlanta Hawks player Terance Mann. Mann quickly dismissed the claims."Ashlyn is cool, people. I don't know why everybody is on her head about anything. She's cool. Me and Ash are cool."He also urged people to stop feeding false narratives and said they should “calm the narrative down.”Castro herself later spoke about the ongoing speculation, batting back at the online gossip about her dating history, saying she’d had “three boyfriends in the last eight years.”Interest in Bellingham’s personal life has only grown as he has continued to shine for England on the biggest stage in football. But despite the headlines about her past, Castro has largely opted to stay out of the public eye, preferring to cheer on the Real Madrid and England midfielder from the stands.
Article Summary: Jude Bellingham's girlfriend, Ashlyn Castro, has attracted attention during the World Cup. She was previously linked to Hollywood actor Michael B. Jordan. She has denied dating rumors surrounding her personal life, insisting she has had only "three boyfriends over the last eight years"

Article Text:

Which teams are interested in Maxx Crosby?

What are insiders saying about the NFL trade rumor?

Contract details and implications

Why could the San Francisco 49ers or Dallas Cowboys make sense?

The latest NFL trade rumor has once again put Las Vegas Raiders star Maxx Crosby at the center of speculation ahead of training camp. While the Raiders signed Crosby to a three-year, $106.5 million extension in 2025, one NFL analyst believes the San Francisco 49ers could become a serious trade destination if the situation changes during the season.The latest Raiders trade rumor links Maxx Crosby with the San Francisco 49ers if the Raiders decide to move him later this year. Brad Gagnon of Bleacher Report believes San Francisco could pursue the five-time Pro Bowler to strengthen its defensive front.The report comes even though Crosby recently signed a new contract with Las Vegas. There is no indication that the Raiders are actively shopping him, but trade speculation continues because of the team's long-term rebuilding plans.Last Word on Sports also listed the Dallas Cowboys as another possible destination. That proposal was not based on insider reporting, but on how well Crosby could fit in Dallas if the Raiders eventually listened to offers.Brad Gagnon believes San Francisco has a reason to explore adding another elite pass rusher despite already having Nick Bosa. Pairing the two could give the 49ers one of the league's strongest defensive lines.Last Word on Sports writer Anthony Palacios also explored several hypothetical trade packages. Among them was a proposal involving the Cowboys, who were previously linked with Crosby earlier in the offseason. Palacios suggested Dallas could build an offer around draft picks and young talent if Las Vegas ever became open to negotiations.For now, though, there has been no report suggesting the Raiders are actively discussing a trade.Maxx Crosby signed a three-year, $106.5 million extension with the Las Vegas Raiders in 2025. The deal includes $91.5 million in guarantees and keeps him under contract through the 2029 season. He is scheduled to earn a $30 million base salary in 2026 while carrying a cap hit of nearly $35.8 million.Because of that contract, any team interested in Crosby would likely need to offer significant draft compensation or established players. Unless the Raiders decide to change direction during the season, moving one of the NFL's best pass rushers would not be an easy decision.The San Francisco 49ers have consistently built their defense around an elite pass rush. Adding Maxx Crosby alongside Nick Bosa would give San Francisco another proven game-changing edge rusher as it pushes for another Super Bowl.The Dallas Cowboys could also make sense if they decide to make another aggressive move before the trade deadline. Crosby would give Dallas another elite defender to pair with its current front and strengthen a defense built around creating pressure.At this stage, the Raiders have not indicated that Crosby is available. Still, with training camp approaching and trade rumors continuing around the league, his name remains one to watch if Las Vegas decides to reshape its roster later in the season.
Article Summary: The latest Raiders trade rumor links Maxx Crosby with the San Francisco 49ers if the Raiders decide to move him later this year. Brad Gagnon of Bleacher Report believes San Francisco could pursue the five-time Pro Bowler to strengthen its defensive front. There is no indication that the Raiders are actively shopping him, but trade speculation continues because of the team's long-term rebuilding plans.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Heraclitus: 'No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man' and a lesson on how change is the only constant
Article Text:
Quote of the by Greek philosopher Heraclitus on change.

"No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man,"

Nothing remains fixed: not the world around us, not the people we meet, and not even our own thoughts, emotions, or identities.

Metaphor of river

Change is the only constant: in relationships, in society

Practice mindfulness

If everything changes, how can we still recognize anything as the same? Why do we still call it the same river?

Quote of the day: Heraclitus was a Greek philosopher who lived around 500 BCE in the city of Ephesus. Unlike philosophers who searched for stability or permanence, Heraclitus believed that the universe is defined by continuous motion. He is often associated with the phrase panta rhei "everything flows" although those exact words do not appear in his surviving writings. His philosophy revolved around the idea that reality is like a river: always moving, always transforming. To him, fire symbolized this eternal process of change because fire is never static; it exists only by constantly consuming and transforming what it touches.His famous saying,is one of the most enduring observations in the history of philosophy. Though only a single sentence, it captures a profound truth about life: everything is in a constant state of change.The quote is not merely about rivers. It is about existence itself. Heraclitus invites us to recognize that permanence is an illusion and that change is the only true constant.The river in Heraclitus' quote is a powerful metaphor because a river appears to be the same from day to day. We call it by the same name, we recognize its banks, and we think of it as a permanent feature of the landscape. Yet the water flowing through it is never the same. Every second, new water replaces the old. The river's depth changes with rainfall, its current shifts, sediment is carried downstream, fish migrate, plants grow along its banks, and erosion subtly reshapes its course. The river preserves its identity while constantly changing its substance.Human beings are much the same. We think of ourselves as the same person we were five or ten years ago because we retain our name, memories, and sense of identity. But in reality, we are constantly changing. Our bodies replace cells, our experiences alter our beliefs, our relationships reshape our personalities, and our successes and failures influence our outlook on life. Even if we return to the exact same place years later, we experience it differently because we ourselves are no longer the person who first stood there.Picture someone revisiting their childhood home after twenty years. The house may look nearly identical. The trees may still stand in the yard, and the familiar streets may evoke old memories. Yet the experience is entirely different because the visitor has accumulated decades of life—education, heartbreaks, achievements, disappointments, friendships, perhaps parenthood. The house has also changed in subtle ways. Paint has faded, neighbors have moved away, new families have arrived, and time has left its marks. The encounter is between two transformed realities: a changed place and a changed person.This insight explains why memories often feel different when revisited. A movie that seemed extraordinary in adolescence may appear simplistic in adulthood. A book that once felt boring may suddenly reveal profound wisdom years later. The work itself has not changed, but the reader has. Life experience provides new lenses through which we interpret the same material. Every encounter becomes a fresh meeting rather than a repetition.The quote also carries an important lesson about relationships. We often wish people would remain exactly as we remember them. Parents hope their children will never grow up. Friends try to preserve old dynamics despite changing careers, marriages, or responsibilities. Romantic partners sometimes expect each other to remain the same individuals they fell in love with years earlier. Heraclitus reminds us that such expectations are unrealistic. People inevitably evolve. Healthy relationships recognize this reality and adapt instead of resisting it.The same principle applies to society. Nations, cultures, economies, and technologies are never static. The world our grandparents knew differs profoundly from the one we inhabit today. Entire industries emerge while others disappear. Political systems rise and fall. Scientific discoveries overturn long-held assumptions. Social values evolve across generations. Attempts to freeze society at one particular moment often fail because history itself is a flowing river.The quote also encourages mindfulness. Since no moment can ever be repeated exactly, every experience becomes uniquely valuable. Watching a sunset, sharing dinner with loved ones, celebrating a milestone, or simply taking a walk all occur under conditions that will never exist again in precisely the same form. The people present will grow older. Our own perspective will evolve. Even if we return tomorrow, it will not truly be the same moment. Recognizing this impermanence deepens our appreciation for the present.At the same time, the quote challenges the common belief that people cannot change. Society often labels individuals based on past mistakes or previous identities. Heraclitus would reject such fixed judgments. A person who was irresponsible at twenty may become dependable at forty. Someone who lacked confidence may develop remarkable courage. Because human beings are always changing, no single moment defines an entire life. This insight underlies ideas of personal growth, forgiveness, and redemption.There is also a subtle paradox hidden within the quote.Heraclitus suggests that identity does not require absolute permanence. Instead, continuity exists through an ongoing process of change. The river remains "the river" not because its water is identical but because its flowing pattern continues. Human identity works similarly. We remain ourselves not because every part of us is unchanged but because our lives form a continuous narrative through constant transformation.Many later philosophers engaged with Heraclitus' ideas. Some disagreed, arguing that there must be permanent truths beneath appearances. Others expanded his insights into discussions about time, consciousness, and existence. Even today, psychologists, scientists, and writers continue to explore the tension between continuity and change that Heraclitus identified more than two and a half millennia ago.
Article Summary: Heraclitus was a Greek philosopher who lived around 500 BCE in the city of Ephesus. His philosophy revolved around the idea that reality is like a river: always moving, always transforming. Heraclitus invites us to recognize that permanence is an illusion and change is the only true constant.

Article Title: Seahawks face a $51 million Rashid Shaheed dilemma that could reshape their receiving corps before training camp
Article Text:
Seattle Seahawks NFL (Getty Images)

Why could Rashid Shaheed become a much bigger part of Seattle's offense?

How does Brian Fleury's offense fit Rashid Shaheed's strengths?

Should the Seahawks reduce Rashid Shaheed's return duties?

The Seattle Seahawks already know Rashid Shaheed can change games on special teams. What remains uncertain is how much more they can ask from him on offense without reducing the impact that made him such a valuable addition. After signing Shaheed to a multiyear, $51 million contract following last season's Super Bowl run, Seattle enters training camp with a different challenge. The focus is no longer on whether he belongs in the offense. It is about finding the right balance between expanding his workload as a receiver and protecting one of the league's most dangerous return specialists.The Athletic recently identified Shaheed as Seattle's top breakout candidate for 2026, but the prediction is not built on the idea that he will suddenly become a classic No. 1 receiver. Instead, it reflects the belief that the Seahawks have yet to unlock everything he can offer within their offense.Seattle traded for Shaheed from the New Orleans Saints in November 2025, giving up fourth and fifth-round draft picks before committing to him with a long-term extension in March. His production after arriving was solid rather than spectacular. He finished with 15 catches for 188 yards across nine regular-season games while adding seven rushing attempts for 64 yards.Those numbers tell only part of the story.Shaheed brings a skill set that neither Jaxon Smith-Njigba nor Cooper Kupp fully provides. Smith-Njigba has developed into the focal point of Seattle's passing game, while Kupp remains one of the league's smartest route runners despite dealing with injuries over recent seasons.Shaheed stretches defenses differently. His speed forces safeties to respect the deep ball, creating more space underneath for teammates. Seattle also has the flexibility to manufacture touches through jet sweeps, motion and quick passes rather than relying solely on vertical routes. His 31-yard jet sweep late last season offered a glimpse of how dangerous he can be when the offense creates opportunities beyond traditional passing concepts.New offensive coordinator Brian Fleury inherits an offense that already suits Shaheed's strengths.After spending seven years with the San Francisco 49ers, Fleury has made it clear he wants his offense to be "fast, violent and aggressive." At the same time, he has stressed that he does not intend to abandon the foundation established under Klint Kubiak.That continuity matters.Shaheed already understands many of the movement-based concepts introduced by Kubiak, who also coached him in New Orleans. Instead of rebuilding his role, Fleury can expand it by placing him in more creative situations before the snap.Although Seattle's depth chart lists Shaheed behind Cooper Kupp at one receiver position, that designation may not accurately reflect his weekly impact. Modern offenses depend less on static alignments and more on creating favorable matchups through motion and personnel groupings.If Fleury regularly deploys Shaheed alongside Kupp and Smith-Njigba, Seattle gains another way to stress opposing defenses without dramatically changing its offensive identity.A larger offensive role naturally creates another question that could shape Seattle's season.In 12 appearances, including the playoffs, Shaheed returned three kicks for touchdowns, highlighted by a 95-yard kickoff return that set the tone in Seattle's divisional playoff win over San Francisco. Few players in the NFL offer that kind of game-changing ability on special teams.At the same time, asking him to handle a growing offensive workload while continuing as both the primary kickoff and punt returner carries obvious risks over a full 17-game season.Seattle's coaching staff now faces a difficult choice. Removing him from return duties would take away one of the team's biggest weapons. Leaving everything on his plate could increase wear and limit his availability when games matter most.That decision may ultimately define Shaheed's season as much as his receiving numbers. He does not need to reach 1,000 receiving yards for the breakout label to fit. If he becomes a dependable third option, consistently creates explosive plays and continues forcing defenses to account for his speed, Seattle's investment will already look justified.
Article Summary: Seattle traded for Rashid Shaheed from the New Orleans Saints in November 2025. He finished with 15 catches for 188 yards across nine regular-season games. Shaheed brings a skill set that neither Jaxon Smith-Njigba nor Cooper Kupp fully provides.

Article Title: How did Lamine Yamal's grandmother sneaking onto a bus from Morocco alone change the entire family's destiny forever?
Article Text:
Who is Lamine Yamal's grandmother and what is her real story? (Via Instagram)

Who is Lamine Yamal's grandmother and what is her real story?

How did Lamine Yamal's parents meet and what was his childhood really like?

What was the most painful moment in Lamine Yamal's personal life off the pitch?

Behind every generational talent is a story that started long before they were born. For Lamine Yamal, that story begins not at La Masia, not in a Barcelona training session, but on a bus leaving Morocco decades ago, carrying a woman with no ticket, no documentation, and five children left behind. Fátima, Yamal's paternal grandmother, left Morocco alone, without documentation, and boarded a bus she had no legal right to be on. That single desperate decision quietly set in motion a chain of events that would eventually produce the most exciting young footballer on the planet.Fátima was the first to arrive on his father's side. She sneaked onto a bus from Morocco, stopped in Algeciras and Granada, and eventually reached Mataró in Catalonia. She had nothing when she arrived. No safety net, no community to fall back on, no financial cushion. What she did have was a willingness to work herself into the ground until she could afford to bring the rest of her family across.She started working three shifts so her son Mounir could come, because he had stayed behind in Morocco, and when she had saved enough money, she paid a woman to bring Mounir and his sister across, who were just three years old at the time.Think about that for a moment. A grandmother working three jobs in a foreign country, alone, so she could eventually send money back to pay someone to transport her own toddlers safely into Spain. That is not background detail. That is the foundation on which Lamine Yamal's entire life was built.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.Yamal spoke about this publicly for the first time in a 2025 podcast with José Ramón de la Morena, and the way he recalled it made clear it was not just family trivia to him. It had shaped how he understood sacrifice, identity, and what it means to carry a name that means something.“My grandmother sneaked onto the bus from Morocco and managed to get to Mataró. She started working three shifts so my father could come because he stayed in Morocco, and when my grandmother made some money, she paid a woman to bring my father and his sister, who came when they were 3 years old,” he said.Fátima is now in her 70s and still lives in the same Rocafonda neighbourhood where she first settled. She has been to matches at the Estadi Olímpic and at the Nou Camp. She has been to Morocco to watch him play for Spain. She was there almost every time it mattered.Lamine Yamal's mother, Sheila, came to Barcelona from Guinea with her own mother, and his parents met in Spain, after which the family started living in a residence for young parents that functioned like a shared dining hall. Two immigrants from completely different parts of Africa, meeting in a Catalan city they had both arrived in through sheer necessity, raising a child in a shared housing facility. It is the kind of origin story that does not appear in most football documentaries.Sheila Ebana had given birth to Yamal at just 16 years old, something Yamal himself confirmed in a 2026 radio interview. She was not supposed to have the resources or the time to manage it all. She was a single parent working in fast food, raising a child while getting him to training, enrolling him in school, and keeping a stable home in a city where she had little support structure. The La Masia commute from Granollers, after his parents separated, was not a minor inconvenience. It was a logistical undertaking for a woman stretched thin in every direction.His parents separated when he was three, although both remained present through his childhood. His mother moved to Granollers while Mounir and grandmother Fátima stayed in Rocafonda, and Yamal would alternate between both cities to spend time with each parent. That is two cities, two households, two sets of routines, all while a child was supposed to be focused enough to develop into a professional footballer. The mental load of that alone is something most people do not factor into discussions about Yamal's composure.As Yamal himself described it, the family always lived in the typical way where a friend would lend them a room, and that is where they stayed. No permanent address, no security, no guarantee that next month would look like this month. And yet, from that uncertainty, came someone who now plays with a kind of freedom and calm that baffles coaches and opponents alike.“After that, we always lived the typical way where a friend has a house and lets you use a room, until my parents separated. Then he went to live with my grandmother and my mother with me in Granollers,” Yamal said.The story of how Yamal got his name is one of the most heartwarming details in the family history. Two men named Lamine and Yamal had been of great help to the family just before his birth, when they needed financial assistance. His parents decided to merge the two names and give them to their son. It is a quiet tribute that nobody on the pitch knows about and nobody in the crowd sees, but every time his name is announced in a stadium, it echoes the kindness of two strangers who helped a struggling young couple when they had nowhere else to turn.In August 2024, Mounir Nasraoui was stabbed several times in a parking lot in the Catalan town of Mataró. He was hospitalized with serious injuries after the attack, and speaking publicly after the incident, he admitted how close he came to losing his life, saying: "I saw myself between life and death."Yamal learned about it while sitting in a car. He had CarPlay on and his cousin, who was in Morocco, called him. He was 17 years old. His father was in intensive care. And he was days away from the start of his first full senior season at Barcelona.Relatives locked him in his house to prevent him from rushing to the scene. That detail matters. It was not a composed athlete deciding to stay calm and focus on football. It was a teenager being physically kept indoors by people who knew he would not make a rational decision if left to himself. That is the version of Yamal that the public rarely gets to see, and it is the version that explains a lot about the player who walks out looking unshakeable every week.He played anyway, days later. Scored. Performed. Said nothing publicly until September 2025, when he finally opened up about how much it had cost him emotionally. The fact that he calls the stabbing the most painful memory of his life, over every difficult match, every injury, every defeat, is the most honest thing he has ever said about himself.His mother, who once transferred between McDonald's branches to keep him in training, had a house bought for her by her son in September 2025. Fátima, who once sneaked onto a bus with nothing, sat inside FC Barcelona's headquarters and watched her grandson sign one of the most significant contracts in the club's recent history. And Mounir, who nearly died in a car park in the neighbourhood where Lamine grew up, recovered fully and continues to attend every match he can, posting his son's goals on social media with visible, unfiltered pride.The "304" celebration Yamal throws after every goal is not just a postcode. It is a direct acknowledgement that Rocafonda, a working-class immigrant neighbourhood most of Spain had never heard of, produced something the world is still trying to fully understand. The gesture is for his grandmother, his father, his mother, and every family that crossed a border with nothing and built a life anyway.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal's paternal grandmother left Morocco alone, without documentation, and boarded a bus she had no legal right to be on. She sneaked onto a bus from Morocco, stopped in Algeciras and Granada, and eventually reached Mataró in Catalonia. She had nothing when she arrived. No safety net, no community to fall back on, no financial cushion. What she did have was a willingness to work herself into the ground until she could afford to bring the rest of her family across.

Article Title: Lamine Yamal scripts European football history after France win with record no one has ever matched
Article Text:
Spain's Lamine Yamal (19) reacts during the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Portugal and Spain in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Monday, July 6, 2026. (AP Photo/Tony Gutierrez)

Twelve starts, twelve wins: a record no European player has matched

Euro 2024 announced Yamal to the world

World Cup campaign has showcased a different side of his game

Spain's Lamine Yamal celebrates after scoring his side's opening goal during the World Cup Group H soccer match between Spain and Saudi Arabia in Atlanta, Sunday, June 21, 2026. (AP Photo/Erik S.Lesser)

More than goals and assists

Spain's march into the 2026 FIFA World Cup final has produced another remarkable milestone for teenage sensation Lamine Yamal. La Roja's 2-0 victory over France in the semi-finals ensured that every one of Yamal's 12 starts across the FIFA World Cup and UEFA European Championship has ended in victory, giving him a perfect 100 percent winning record when named in Spain's starting XI at the two biggest international tournaments. No European player has ever started as many World Cup and European Championship matches while maintaining an unblemished record, underlining the extraordinary impact the Barcelona forward has already made before even reaching his 20th birthday.International football rarely allows room for perfection, particularly at major tournaments where every knockout match can define a generation.Yet Yamal has achieved precisely that.Spain's victory over France in Dallas marked his 12th career start across the UEFA European Championship and FIFA World Cup combined. All 12 have ended in Spanish victories, giving the teenager a flawless 12-0 record whenever he has been selected from the opening whistle.It is the best 100 percent winning record ever recorded by a European player across the two competitions when starting matches.The statistic reflects more than individual brilliance. Across those 12 starts, Spain have consistently produced their best football with Yamal on the right flank, where his movement, ball retention and creativity have become central to Luis de la Fuente's attacking structure.The remarkable sequence began during UEFA Euro 2024. Fresh from his breakthrough season with Barcelona, Yamal established himself as one of the tournament's standout performers as Spain won the European Championship with a perfect seven victories from seven matches.He started six matches during that campaign, contributing one goal and four assists.His defining moment came in the semi-final against France, where his spectacular long-range strike levelled the contest before Spain completed the comeback. Days later, he helped create the opening goal for Nico Williams in Spain's 2-1 victory over England in the final.Those performances transformed him from one of Europe's brightest prospects into one of world football's biggest stars, culminating in an astonishing second-place finish in the Ballon d'Or voting at just 18 years of age.Yamal arrived at the 2026 World Cup after recovering from a hamstring injury suffered in April, an issue that disrupted his preparations for the tournament.While his individual numbers have been more modest than those of Golden Boot contenders Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi, or England midfielder Jude Bellingham, his influence has remained unmistakable.He has started all six of Spain's World Cup matches, taking his overall tally to 12 consecutive winning starts at major tournaments.His only goal of the tournament came against Saudi Arabia, when he opened the scoring in Spain's 4-0 victory. The strike made him the youngest player to score the opening goal in a World Cup match since Pelé in 1958, while also making him Spain's second-youngest World Cup goalscorer behind Gavi.Opponents have increasingly committed multiple defenders to containing the Barcelona winger, limiting his direct goal contributions but creating valuable space for teammates.That influence was evident again in the semi-final against France. Yamal's intelligent run into the penalty area drew Lucas Digne into the foul that allowed Mikel Oyarzabal to convert the opening penalty before Pedro Porro sealed a 2-0 victory in the second half.Across his 12 starts at the World Cup and European Championship, Yamal has contributed two goals and four assists.His broader impact, however, extends well beyond those numbers.At Euro 2024, he was Spain's primary creative outlet, while at the World Cup he has increasingly become the player around whom opponents reshape their entire defensive structure. His ability to attract double teams has opened central spaces for players such as Dani Olmo, Pedro Porro and Mikel Oyarzabal to exploit.Spain's latest victory also extended the national team's unbeaten run to 37 matches, equalling Italy's all-time European record, while making La Roja the first European nation ever to win eight consecutive knockout matches across the World Cup and European Championship.As Spain prepare for the World Cup final, Yamal will not only be chasing the biggest trophy of his career. He will also be attempting to preserve one of international football's most remarkable records—a perfect 12 victories from 12 starts on the biggest stages the sport has to offer.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal has made 12 starts across the FIFA World Cup and UEFA European Championship combined. No European player has ever started as many matches while maintaining an unblemished record. The 19-year-old has scored two goals and made four assists in those 12 starts.

Article Title: "That's difficult for me...": Kylian Mbappé's girlfriend Ester Expósito breaks silence on the tough World Cup choice she had to make
Article Text:
The French star player Kylian Mbappé has been a force to be reckoned with on the field, impressing everyone around the world. While his record breaking performance has impressed billions across the world, his high profile relationship with Spanish actress Ester Expósito has turned heads. As the star player plays against the nation of his girlfriend, Ester Expósito has opened up about it.

Kylian Mbappé’s girlfriend Ester Expósito opens up about the difficult choice she had to make during the 2026 World Cup



Kylian Mbappé opened up about the passion the french team had for the 2026 World Cup



The French star player Kylian Mbappé has been a force to be reckoned with on the field. While his record breaking performance has impressed billions across the world, his high profile relationship with Spanish actress Ester Expósito has turned heads. As the star player plays against the nation of his girlfriend, Ester Expósito has opened up about it.Kylian Mbappé A few weeks ago, as the popular actress Ester Expósito turned heads at the Monte-Carlo TV Festival, she opened up about who she would be supporting in the 2026 World Cup.In an interview with the New York Post, Kylian Mbappé’s girlfriend, Ester Expósito, said, “Oh, no! That’s a difficult choice for me… Who knows?”Both the Real Madrid star player Kylian Mbappé and the popular Spanish actress Ester Expósito have been strictly private about their high profile relationship even though the two have often been spotted in public.Last month, before the 2026 World Cup began, many criticised the Real Madrid star player Kylian Mbappé for going on lavish vacations in Ibiza and Paris with his girlfriend.Despite the trolling, Kylian Mbappé and his girlfriend, Ester Expósito have remained distant from all the drama concerning their relationship as he kept his focus on the World Cup.Kylian Mbappé’ recorded an impressive performance on the field with 8 goals and providing 3 assists in 6 matches.A few days ago, in a heartfelt interview with French magazine Gazeta, the star player Kylian Mbappé’ opened up about his team.The French captain said, “We are aware of our potential. But what we are demonstrating here is more important. It's not the opponents' fear that makes you win. Those are just feelings. We still have a lot to prove before we are considered invincible and deserve all the other superlatives you give us."The Real Madrid star forward aded, “The passion to represent France, and to play in a World Cup in this shirt. There's nothing stronger. Within the squad, we are perfectly aware of this and try to teach it to the younger players too. We are a team and a country with an important history in the World Cup.”He continued, “The youngsters need to know this and understand what it means to handle the pressure of playing for France."While there have been many speculations about his whirlwind romance, Kylian Mbappé’ has kept the focus on his career as he has refused to address the romance rumors in public during the 2026 World Cup.

Everything you need to know about Fortnite Odyssey Cup



Odyssey Cup date and time



How to register for The Odyssey Cup



All free rewards in Fortnite Odyssey Cup



Reward Requirement Odysseus (Outfit) Top placement by region Odysseus' Xiphos (Pickaxe) Top placement by region Bow of Iphytus (Back Bling) Top placement by region King's Helm (Spray) 40 points The Trojan War (Loading Screen) 8 points

Odysseus skin in Fortnite Item Shop



Christopher Nolan’s highly anticipated film adaptation of Homer’s The Odyssey is hitting theatres on July 17, 2026, and Epic Games is celebrating in style. The Odyssey crossover is officially coming to Fortnite, bringing Matt Damon’s take on the legendary hero Odysseus to the Battle Royale island.Before the official introduction of the skin, Epic Games is providing you with the opportunity to get your hands on it for completely free. They are organizing the Odyssey Cup, participating in which could get you a variety of cosmetics.In the section below, we take a detailed look at the upcoming Odyssey Cup in Fortnite.The Odyssey Cup is a limited-time competitive event in Fortnite that gives you the opportunity to earn the Odysseus skin and other themed cosmetics without spending any V-Bucks. It follows the same format as previous free-skin competitive cups in Fortnite, where you must rank high enough in their region to claim the rewards.The cup is available in both Battle Royale and Zero Build modes, and Epic is also running a dedicated Mobile variant in the Reload mode, meaning iPhone and iPad players returning to Fortnite through the iOS App Store can also participate.The Odyssey Cup takes place across July 15–16, 2026. You can find the full schedule and registration details inside the in-game Compete tab, where the exact session times for each region and game mode are listed.Registering for The Odyssey Cup requires no additional sign-up outside of Fortnite itself. Simply open the game, navigate to the Compete tab from the lobby, and select The Odyssey Cup from the list of active tournaments. From there, you can view your region's session times and join a match during the active window. Two-factor authentication must be enabled on your Epic Games account before you can participate in any competitive Fortnite event.Here is everything players can earn from The Odyssey Cup and the placement or point requirements needed to unlock each reward:The exact number of qualifying placements varies by region. In Europe, the top 1,000 players earn the skin, while in NA Central it is the top 700, NA West top 200, Brazil top 200, and Oceania, Asia, and Middle East each have a top 100 threshold. Article Summary: Odysseus skin will be available in Fortnite Item Shop on July 17, 2026. Epic Games is organizing The Odyssey Cup, a free-skin competitive event. The cup is available in both Battle Royale and Zero Build modes. The top 1,000 players in each region will earn the OdysseUS skin.

Article Title: Who is Charles Snowden? Cowboys star suspended for first three games of 2026 NFL season following DUI case
Article Text:
Who is Charles Snowden? Cowboys star suspended for first three games of 2026 NFL season following DUI case(Getty Images)



Who is Charles Snowden?

Why was Charles Snowden suspended?

Dallas Cowboys defensive end Charles Snowden will begin the 2026 NFL season with a three-game suspension after the NFL disciplined him following the resolution of his 2024 DUI case. The league announced the suspension through its official transactions wire on Tuesday, and an NFL spokesperson confirmed to Fox News Digital that the punishment stemmed from Snowden's DUI arrest while he was with the Las Vegas Raiders.Snowden is still eligible to participate in the Cowboys' training camp and all three preseason games. His suspension will take effect after the NFL's final 53-man roster deadline, forcing him to miss Dallas' regular-season opener against the New York Giants, followed by games against the Washington Commanders and Baltimore Ravens. As of now, the Dallas Cowboys have not publicly commented on the NFL's decision.Charles Snowden is a 28-year-old defensive end who currently plays for the Dallas Cowboys. A native of Silver Spring, Maryland, he starred at the University of Virginia, where he developed into one of the ACC's top pass rushers.His father is Chuck Snowden. Chuck Snowden, along with his wife Terri, raised Charles in Silver Spring, Maryland, and supported his athletic journey from Blake High School through his college career at the University of Virginia.In his senior season in 2020, Snowden led the Cavaliers with six sacks and 10 tackles for loss before earning second-team All-ACC honors despite suffering a season-ending ankle injury.After going undrafted in the 2021 NFL Draft, Snowden signed with the Chicago Bears as a free agent. He later spent time with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers before joining the Las Vegas Raiders, where he earned the most significant playing time of his NFL career.The 2025 season proved to be Snowden's breakthrough campaign. He appeared in 15 games, making nine starts, and recorded career highs with three sacks, 28 total tackles and five tackles for loss. He also intercepted the first pass of his NFL career. Across 33 career games, Snowden has totaled 4.5 sacks, 28 tackles and one interception.Although he re-signed with the Raiders in April 2026, Las Vegas released him less than a month later. The Cowboys signed Snowden on June 18 after he impressed the coaching staff during a minicamp tryout, adding experienced depth to their pass-rushing unit.Charles Snowden's suspension stems from a DUI arrest in Las Vegas on December 9, 2024. In January 2026, he pleaded no contest to a misdemeanor DUI charge as part of a plea agreement. Under the agreement, he was ordered to pay a $1,000 fine and complete DUI and coroner's education courses. If he fulfills those requirements, the misdemeanor DUI charge will be reduced to reckless driving.Although the NFL's transactions wire did not specify the reason for the suspension, an NFL spokesperson confirmed it was related to Snowden's resolved DUI case. Under the league's Personal Conduct Policy, discipline is often imposed after legal proceedings have concluded.The suspension delays Charles Snowden's Cowboys debut in meaningful games. Still, he will be available throughout training camp and the preseason before becoming eligible to return after serving the three-game ban at the start of the regular season.
Article Summary: Charles Snowden is a 28-year-old defensive end who currently plays for the Dallas Cowboys. His suspension stems from a DUI arrest in Las Vegas on December 9, 2024. In January 2026, he pleaded no contest to a misdemeanor DUI charge as part of a plea agreement. He was ordered to pay a $1,000 fine and complete DUI and coroner's education courses.

Article Title: Shubman Gill's India ends England's 12-year home stronghold at Edgbaston with six-wicket win
Article Text:
India end England's 12-year old streak (Image: BCCI)

Date Opponent Result September 2, 2014 India Lost by 9 wickets June 9, 2015 New Zealand Won by 210 runs June 24, 2016 Sri Lanka Won by 10 wickets June 10, 2017 Australia Won by 40 runs (DLS) June 30, 2019 India Won by 31 runs July 11, 2019 Australia Won by 8 wickets July 13, 2021 Pakistan Won by 3 wickets May 29, 2025 West Indies Won by 238 runs July 14, 2026 India Lost by 6 wickets

England's collapse after strong start



India complete comfortable chase



India brought an end to England's remarkable 12-year unbeaten run in Men's ODIs at Edgbaston with a convincing six-wicket victory on 1st ODI.India entered the ODI series on the back of a disappointing 4-0 T20I series defeat and were determined to bounce back. The visitors responded in emphatic fashion, ending England's long-standing winning streak at their home ground. England had remained unbeaten in seven consecutive Men's ODIs at Edgbaston since 2015. The last team to defeat them in a 50-over international at the Birmingham venue was also India, who registered a comprehensive nine-wicket victory on September 2, 2014.Adding to the win, India registered their sixth consecutive ODI victory over England, setting a new record for their longest winning streak against the Three Lions. Their previous best was five successive wins, a feat they had achieved on three separate occasions.The hosts made a promising start, reaching 61 without loss, before India's bowlers triggered a dramatic collapse that left them reeling at 80/5. Joe Root held the innings together with an unbeaten 76, while Liam Dawson added a valuable 68. Their 111-run partnership helped England recover to 258 before they were bowled out. Axar Patel played a pivotal role with the ball, claiming four wickets and breaking the backbone of England's batting line-up with figures of 4/62.India's pursuit was built around another assured knock from captain Shubman Gill , who scored 80 before retiring hurt with cramps. The visitors briefly lost momentum after slipping to 160/4, but Axar Patel and Washington Sundar combined to extinguish England's hopes. The pair stitched together an unbeaten 102-run stand, with Axar finishing on 57* and Sundar remaining unbeaten on 52, as India sealed victory with 28 deliveries remaining.Also this is only the second instance of India successfully chasing down a 250+ target in ODIs since 2016 where either Rohit or Kohli failed to get to 50; the other instance came against England at Old Trafford in 2022 when they chased down 260, with both Rohit & Kohli scoring 17 runs each.
Article Summary: England had remained unbeaten in seven consecutive Men's ODIs at Edgbaston since 2015. The last team to defeat them in a 50-over international at the Birmingham venue was also India, who registered a comprehensive nine-wicket victory on September 2, 2014. Joe Root held the innings together with an unbeaten 76, while Liam Dawson added 68.

Article Title: Who are Michael Olise’s parents? All about Vincent and Mina Olise
Article Text:
Who are Michael Olise's parents (Getty Images)

Who is Mina Olise and why did she influence Michael's decision to play for France?

Who is Vincent Olise and what did he do before Michael's football career?

Does Michael Olise have a sibling and who introduced him to chess?

Michael Olise is one of the more compelling stories at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Not just for what he does on the ball, but for the background he carries into every match. Born in England to a Nigerian father and a Franco-Algerian mother, Olise had four countries he could have played for internationally. He chose France, the nation his mother, Mina, comes from. That decision didn't happen in isolation. It was shaped by two parents who gave him a world in miniature before he ever set foot on a professional pitch.Mina is French, and that connection was never just a line on a passport. Olise has said that growing up, his household ran on two languages at once. His father, Vincent, spoke English with him, while Mina spoke French. Michael answered both in English but stayed equally at home in French. The family made regular trips to Algeria, Nigeria, and France, so none of these identities felt distant or theoretical to him.When he joined the French national team in 2024, he was candid about the pull he felt toward his mother's country. "My mother is from France. When I was little, I went to France. I had this connection with France. I'm proud to be here," he told reporters.For Olise, picking France wasn't about picking the most convenient option. It was personal.Michael's father, Vincent, has his own sporting biography. He represented Nigeria's national cricket team from 1983 to 1990 and continued playing at club level well beyond that. He operated as an opening batsman and specialist fielder, and as of 2020, he held the joint record for 10 wickets in a single innings.Michael has cited cricket as one of the games he genuinely loves, and the sport makes regular appearances when he talks about his hobbies. Alongside chess and ping-pong, cricket connects him to his father's world in a way football never quite does. "We never talk about football, to be honest. We are both in it so much. We talk about life," he told Highsnobiety in June 2026.It's a telling detail. Vincent passed down a competitive instinct to his son but didn't confine it to one sport.Michael has a younger brother, Richard Olise, who was on Chelsea FC's books before a recent release. The two are close enough to be competitive off the pitch. Michael has been open about the fact that he absolutely hates losing to Richard. "I never want to lose to my little brother. Or my mom," he said. That competitive edge with family is familiar to anyone who's grown up with siblings, and it humanises an otherwise composed footballer who rarely shows much emotion during a game.The chess detail is worth noting on its own. Mina introduced Michael to the game when he was young, and he's kept at it, currently playing at a rating between 1300 and 1400. It fits with how he speaks about challenges in general. "When you have a setback, take it as a lesson. Use it as time to grow," he told Highsnobiety. That kind of thinking doesn't emerge from nowhere. For Olise, it seems to have started at home.
Article Summary: Michael Olise is one of the more compelling stories at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Born in England to a Nigerian father and a Franco-Algerian mother, Olise had four countries he could have played for internationally. He chose France, the nation his mother, Mina, comes from. His father, Vincent, played cricket for Nigeria from 1983 to 1990.
